pattern: Bounded simulation: repeatedly consume a fixed batch from a primary resource and, whenever a batch completes, pull a single top-up unit from a secondary resource if any remains.
hints: Simulate the process directly: every time the main resource drops by the batch size, check whether a top-up is available and apply it immediately before continuing.
The main resource can only ever receive as many top-up units as the number of full batches it actually consumes, no more, even if the secondary resource holds far more than that.
Once the main resource can no longer form a full batch, whatever remains still contributes its own share directly with no further top-ups possible.

edge_inputs.description: main tank never reaches a full batch regardless of the additional tank size
exactly one full batch, triggering exactly one top-up
additional tank has far more than could ever be used, top-ups are still capped by consumption
maximum main tank with minimal additional tank
main tank just short of two full batches
several chained top-ups across many batches

stress.description: random tank sizes across the full allowed range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    mainTank = rng.randint(1, 100)
    additionalTank = rng.randint(1, 100)
    return [mainTank, additionalTank]
